title: Single-File Reach
description: Thread one growing pulse file through the Reach by replaying frontier commands before the line collapses.

howToPlayJson.title: Single-File Reach
howToPlayJson.objective: Survive a 2-4 minute frontier relay by memorizing and replaying pulse commands for three far worlds, building combo score while keeping the single-file transmission from breaking.

controls.input: Tap the next glowing pulse node in order
controls.outcome: Confirms that command and advances the single-file relay

controls.input: Swipe left or right when the file forks
controls.outcome: Routes the next chunk of the sequence to a different world lane for bonus or safety

controls.input: Press and hold on a long-tone node
controls.outcome: Stabilizes heavy commands and preserves combo
Skips one pulse at half multiplier instead of failing instantly

steps: A short pulse sequence scrolls up a single vertical file, with each beat tied to one of three frontier worlds.
The player replays the exact order by tapping and holding matching pulse nodes as they rise into the reach zone.
Every few seconds the file hits a fork: the player must swipe toward one world lane, causing upcoming beats there to score double while the
Unchosen lanes become unstable and may mutate one pulse, forcing a fast adaptation instead of pure repetition.
Sequences lengthen, tempo increases, and mixed tap plus hold commands appear until the player misses twice or the file overload meter fills.
howToPlayJson.scoring: Score comes from each correct pulse, with a combo multiplier that climbs every 5 perfect inputs. Forking into a risky unstable lane gives higher base points. A mistake breaks the combo, and a second unresolved mistake in
tips: Listen first: each world has its own instrument, so audio helps separate similar-looking beats.
Take risky forks early when sequences are short; late-game stability is worth more than greed.
Use the panic seal only to protect a high multiplier from a mutated pulse.
Hold notes are the usual combo killers, so commit your finger early.
